summary: Crystal‑clear SBD/Matrix capture of the legendary June 9 1990 Cal Expo show – a high‑energy set highlighted by Peggy‑O, Victim Or The Crime and an unforgettable Watchtower solo.
review: The matrix recording is frequently described as “the best ever” with pristine clarity and just enough audience ambience to convey the party atmosphere of the Cal Expo run.  Musically the show is praised for its tight first set (Peggy‑O, Mexicali Blues, Loose Lucy) and a second set that includes standout performances of Watchtower, Victim Or The Crime and Stella Blue, with Jerry’s solo after a stage intrusion becoming a legendary moment.  Overall sentiment is overwhelmingly positive.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and extol both the show’s energy and the superb matrix sound quality; only a few low scores focus on unrelated stage‑incident complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Repeatedly called the clearest SBD/Matrix ever, with crystal‑clear instrument separation, balanced mix and just enough crowd noise to convey the live vibe.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note solid, energetic first and second sets; the setlist is considered one of the best of the 1990 summer run with excellent pacing and a high‑octane climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solo after the stage‑intruder incident is described as “ripping” and a highlight; his tone and improvisational fire receive repeated praise.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s presence is noted in a quote about the “Monkey and the Engineer” moment; no specific performance critique.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland receives glowing remarks, called “flawless” and a key part of the show’s magic.
